prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design a panel of 1,000 thermostabilised sequences for the PETase enzyme backbone from Ideonella sakaiensis (PDB 5XJH) to improve catalytic activity at 55°C for industrial PET plastic degradation. Apply disulfide bond constraints at positions that maximise core packing. outline a computational approach covering: (1) backbone preparation and disulfide-bond mask specification in ProteinMPNN, (2) sequence sampling at temperature 0.1–0.5 with 1,000 independent runs, (3) ranking by ProteinMPNN log-likelihood and ESMFold pLDDT, (4) selection of top-20 sequences for Tm prediction with FoldX. Provide guidance on expected sequence diversity and wet-lab validation using differential scanning fluorimetry.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (5XJH); target: PETase poly(ethylene terephthalate) hydrolase (ISF6_4831); organism: Ideonella sakaiensis; catalogue version v1.0
